title: Anoplophytum strobilanthum
taxonomy: Anoplophytum strobilanthum is classified as a species of flowering plant in the asparagus family, Asparagaceae. It belongs to the genus Anoplophytum, which is endemic to the Western Ghats in India. This plant is also known as the Neelakuranji, a name given to it in Malayalam, a language spoken in the region. Within the plant kingdom, Anoplophytum strobilanthum is further classified as a monocot, characterized by the presence of one cotyledon in its seedling stage. The plant is a rare and unique species that blooms only once every 12 years, making it a subject of interest among botanists and horticulturists.
uses: Anoplophytum strobilanthum is a plant native to the Western Ghats in India, and its medicinal uses are primarily based on traditional knowledge.
